headline: Craft Beer Industry Faces RM420M Loss From Excise Tax Hike
context: The 15% excise duty increase on alcoholic beverages in Budget 2026 threatens to eliminate 62 of Malaysia's 84 craft breweries, with the Malaysian Craft Brewers Association estimating RM420 million in industry revenue loss and 2,400 job cuts.

cards.big: 15% excise hike could kill 62 of 84 craft breweries — RM420 million industry facing extinction from blanket sin tax
cards.sub: Craft brewers already pay RM7.40 per litre in excise — among highest in the world — before the 15% increase

cards.big: Malaysia's alcohol excise at RM175 per litre of pure alcohol is 3x Thailand's RM58 and 5x Indonesia's RM35
cards.sub: Legal alcohol market at RM4.2 billion is shrinking while illicit alcohol estimated at RM1.8 billion grows — tax hike worsens this

cards.big: 84 craft breweries employ 2,400 workers and support 180 hospitality venues — predominantly non-Muslim operators in urban areas
cards.sub: Tourism impact: craft brewery trail in KL and Penang attracts 280,000 visitors annually spending RM140 million

cards.big: Excise rate set without industry consultation or regulatory impact assessment — announced in budget speech as fait accompli
cards.sub: No graduated tax structure — micro-breweries producing 50,000 litres pay same rate as multinationals producing 50 million litres

cards.big: Taxing craft breweries at the same rate as multinational giants isn't sin tax policy — it's small business destruction by regulatory laziness
Should Malaysia adopt tiered alcohol excise with 50% reduction for producers below 100,000 litres to protect craft industry viability?
